title: Add After Multiplicative Comparison
description: After finding a multiplicative comparison amount, add an additional specified quantity.
condition: A quantity is described as k times another amount, and then an additional fixed number is added (e.g., '2 more than').
response: Compute k × base amount, then add the additional fixed number.
label: Principle
example: If Mia sells $3 \times 6 = 18$ tickets and James sells 2 more, then $18 + 2 = 20$.

title: Add Multi-Digit Numbers (Standard Algorithm)
description: Add two multi-digit whole numbers using the standard vertical algorithm with place-value alignment and regrouping as needed.
condition: Two whole numbers written vertically with digits aligned by place value (ones, tens, hundreds, etc.).
response: Add digits column by column from right to left, regrouping when a column sum is 10 or more, and record the total.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Includes regrouping across one or more place values.

title: Add Rounded Numbers
description: Add two or more numbers after they have been rounded to a specified place value.
condition: Given two or more rounded whole numbers to combine for an estimate.
response: Compute the sum of the rounded numbers.
label: Production / Skill
example: Round $551 \approx 600$ and $404 \approx 400$, then compute $600 + 400 = 1{,}000$.
notes: Used after rounding to estimate totals.

title: Adjacent Place Value ×10 Relationship
description: Recognize that a digit in one place is 10 times the value of the same digit in the place to its right.
condition: The same digit appears in adjacent place values (e.g., thousands and hundreds).
response: State that the value in the higher place is 10 times the value in the place to its right.
label: Concept
example: In $37{,}581$, the 7 in the thousands place (7,000) is $10$ times the 7 in the hundreds place (700).

title: Commutative Property of Multiplication
description: Multiplication facts can be represented in either factor order with the same product.
condition: A multiplication equation a × b = c is given.
response: Recognize or state that b × a = c represents the same relationship.
label: Principle
example: Since $5 \times 9 = 45$, it is also true that $9 \times 5 = 45$.

title: Compare Estimation Accuracy by Place Value
description: Determine which rounding place value produces the closest estimate to an exact total.
condition: Given the same numbers rounded to different place values and their resulting totals.
response: Select the place value that gives the estimate closest to the actual sum.
label: Principle / Model
example: Exact sum: $551 + 404 = 955$. Rounding to tens gives $550 + 400 = 950$ (difference 5), rounding to hundreds gives $600 + 400 = 1{,}000$ (difference 45). Choose tens.
notes: Requires understanding of absolute difference to judge closeness.

title: Compare Multi-Digit Numbers by Place Value
description: Compare two whole numbers by examining digits from the greatest place value to the least.
condition: Two multi-digit whole numbers are given for comparison.
response: Compare digits starting at the leftmost place; determine and write <, >, or =.
label: Principle
example: Compare $724{,}740$ and $742{,}740$: since $7=7$ and $2<4$ in the ten-thousands place, $724{,}740 < 742{,}740$.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Compute Basic Multiplication Fact
description: Multiply two whole numbers to find their product.
condition: Two whole numbers are given to be multiplied.
response: Calculate their product.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Arithmetic fluency skill.

title: Compute Multiplicative Comparison (How Many Times)
description: Determine how many times as large one quantity is compared to another by division.
condition: Two quantities are given, and the prompt asks 'how many times as many' or to complete '___ times as many'.
response: Divide the larger quantity by the smaller quantity.
label: Principle
example: Since Amy completes 32 problems and Carla completes 8, compute $32 \div 8 = 4$.

title: Convert Kilograms to Grams
description: Convert a mass in kilograms to grams using the metric relationship between the units.
condition: A quantity given in kilograms that needs to be expressed in grams.
response: Multiply the number of kilograms by 1,000 to find the equivalent number of grams.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Uses the fact that 1 kg = 1,000 g.

title: Convert Kilometers to Meters
description: Convert a length in kilometers to meters using the metric relationship between the units.
condition: A quantity given in kilometers that needs to be expressed in meters.
response: Multiply the number of kilometers by 1,000 to find the equivalent number of meters.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Based on the metric base-10 structure.

title: Convert Liters to Milliliters
description: Convert a volume in liters to milliliters using the metric relationship between the units.
condition: A quantity given in liters that needs to be expressed in milliliters.
response: Multiply the number of liters by 1,000 to find the equivalent number of milliliters.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Uses the fact that 1 L = 1,000 mL.

title: Convert Meters to Centimeters
description: Convert a measurement in meters to centimeters using the metric relationship.
condition: A quantity expressed in meters.
response: Multiply the number of meters by 100 to find centimeters.
label: Principle
example: $4$ meters $= 4 \times 100 = 400$ centimeters.
notes: Based on the metric fact that 1 meter = 100 centimeters. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Estimate by Rounding to Nearest Thousand
description: Round multi-digit numbers to the nearest thousand to estimate a sum or difference.
condition: A word problem asks for a closest estimate and involves numbers in the thousands or greater.
response: Round each number to the nearest thousand and compute the approximate result.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Used to select the closest answer choice.

title: Evaluate Metric Conversion Equation
description: Determine whether a given metric unit equation is true or false by converting units and comparing values.
condition: An equation stating two quantities with different metric units are equal.
response: Convert one quantity to the same unit as the other and determine whether the numerical values are equal (state True or False).
label: Principle / Model
example: Check $5 \text{ km} = 500 \text{ m}$: $5 \text{ km} = 5{,}000 \text{ m}$, and $5{,}000 \ne 500$, so False.
notes: Requires correct unit conversion before comparison.

title: Find Missing Addend from Final Total
description: Determine an unknown change amount by subtracting the current amount from the final total.
condition: A situation where a starting amount is changed and a final total is given, and the unknown amount added must be found.
response: Subtract the current amount from the final total to find the missing addend.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires prior computation of remaining bricks.

title: Identify Rounding Threshold Digit
description: Determine whether to round up or down by checking the digit immediately to the right of the target place value.
condition: Given a number and a specified place value to round to.
response: If the next digit is 5 or greater, round up; if less than 5, round down.
label: Rule / Plan
example: To round $218{,}657$ to the nearest thousand, check the hundreds digit (6). Since $6 \ge 5$, round up to $219{,}000$.
notes: Conventional rounding rule.

title: Identify Value of a Digit
description: Determine the value contributed by a digit based on its place in a multi-digit number.
condition: A specific digit in a multi-digit number is indicated.
response: Multiply the digit by its place value (e.g., 7 in thousands place → 7,000).
label: Principle
example: In $37{,}581$, the underlined 7 in the thousands place has value $7 \times 1{,}000 = 7{,}000$.

title: Interpret 'Times as Much' as Multiplication
description: Translate multiplicative comparison language into a multiplication expression.
condition: A statement of the form 'A is n times as much as B.'
response: Represent as $A = n \times B$ (or compute using multiplication).
label: Principle
example: "12 is 4 times as much as 3" means $12 = 4 \times 3$.
notes: Supports equation writing and modeling. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Match Computed Sum to Answer Choice
description: Compute each expression and match it to the correct value from a list of answer choices.
condition: Multiple addition expressions and a list of possible sums are provided.
response: Compute each sum and select the matching value from the choices.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires accurate computation before matching.

title: Model Multiplication as Equal Groups
description: Represent a multiplication situation using equal groups or a visual model.
condition: A multiplicative comparison or product is given.
response: Draw or describe equal groups showing repeated addends to match the product.
label: Production / Skill
example: To show $12 = 4 \times 3$, draw four boxes each labeled 3 to total 12.
notes: Visual modeling skill.

title: Order Numbers Least to Greatest
description: Arrange a set of multi-digit numbers in ascending order by comparing place values.
condition: A list of multi-digit whole numbers is given.
response: Sequence the numbers from smallest to largest based on place value comparison.
label: Principle

title: Regroup Hundreds as Tens
description: Rename 1 hundred as 10 tens during subtraction regrouping.
condition: In subtraction, the tens digit is less than the subtrahend’s tens digit and there are hundreds available to regroup.
response: Decrease the hundreds digit by 1 and increase the tens digit by 10.
label: Principle / Model
example: $7$ hundreds becomes $6$ hundreds $10$ tens.
notes: Conceptual understanding of place value renaming.

title: Round Number to Specified Place Value
description: Round a whole number to the nearest ten, hundred, thousand, ten thousand, or hundred thousand.
condition: Given any whole number and a specified place value for rounding.
response: Apply rounding rule and express the correctly rounded number.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Round $465{,}959$ to the nearest ten thousand: check the thousands digit (5), round up to $470{,}000$.
notes: Integrates threshold identification and zeroing digits.

title: Select Numbers That Round to Target Value
description: Determine which given numbers round to a specified target when rounded to a stated place value.
condition: Given a target rounded value (e.g., 81,000) and several candidate numbers.
response: Identify all numbers that round to the target under the stated rounding rule.
label: Principle / Model
example: Numbers between $80{,}500$ and $81{,}499$ round to $81{,}000$. For example, $80{,}651 \to 81{,}000$.
notes: Requires reasoning about interval boundaries.

title: Subtract Multi-Digit Numbers Using Standard Algorithm
description: Subtract one multi-digit whole number from another using place value alignment and regrouping.
condition: Two multi-digit whole numbers written in vertical form for subtraction.
response: Subtract digits by place from right to left, regrouping as needed.
label: Principle
notes: Includes regrouping across place values.

title: Translate Word Problem into Sequential Operations
description: Convert a multi-step word problem into a sequence of addition and subtraction operations.
condition: A word problem describes an initial quantity, amounts used or removed, and a final total.
response: Represent the situation with numerical expressions in the correct order (subtract amounts used, then relate to final total).
label: Principle / Model
example: Start with $51,975$, subtract $18,133$ and $25,748$, then compare result to $20,000$.
notes: Structural modeling step before computation.

title: Write Number from Expanded Form
description: Combine place value terms written in expanded form to determine the standard form number.
condition: A sum of products of digits and powers of ten (e.g., $8 \times 10{,}000$).
response: Compute each product and add them to write the number in standard form.
label: Principle
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Write Number in Unit Form
description: Express a multi-digit number as a sum of place-value units (e.g., hundreds, thousands).
condition: Given a whole number in standard form.
response: State the number as counts of place-value units.
label: Principle / Model
example: $465{,}392 = 4$ hundred thousands $+ 6$ ten thousands $+ 5$ thousands $+ 3$ hundreds $+ 9$ tens $+ 2$ ones.
notes: Based on base-ten place value structure.

title: Write Number in Word Form
description: Convert a multi-digit whole number from standard form to word form using place value names.
condition: A multi-digit whole number in standard form is given.
response: Write the number in words by grouping into periods (thousands, ones) and naming each place value.
label: Rule
example: $203{,}090$ is written as "two hundred three thousand ninety."
notes: Conventions of English number naming must be memorised.

title: Zero Out Lower Place Values After Rounding
description: After rounding to a specified place, replace all digits to the right with zeros.
condition: Given a number rounded to a specified place value.
response: Keep digits up to the rounding place (adjusted if rounding up) and change all following digits to 0.
label: Production / Skill
example: Rounding $367{,}915$ to the nearest hundred gives $367{,}900$ (tens and ones become 0).
notes: Follows application of rounding decision.
